title: Department of Defense support for "Mission: Impossible -- Dead Reckoning Part One"

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the 2023 movie "Mission: Impossible -- Dead Reckoning Part One" (also known as "Mission: Impossible 7"), directed by Christopher McQuarrie and starring Tom Cruise. I request any and all documents pertaining to DoD support for "Mission: Impossible -- Dead Reckoning Part One." Such documents may include but should not be not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of the DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Mission: Impossible -- Dead Reckoning Part One," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Zackary DeCicco

title: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request the following records related to this agency’s use of any face recognition platform, including but not limited to any system over which this agency does not maintain primary ownership but to which members of the agency have access or any platform that enables face recognition:

1. AGREEMENTS and CONTRACTS
Any and all agreements (including related amendments, attachments, and exhibits) related to the acquisition and use of this "software," algorithm, or program.

2. INSTRUCTIONAL MATERIALS and TRAINING
All instructional materials, presentations and presentation materials and other guidance on the use of "the software." Responsive materials include recorded video and audio, PowerPoint files, prepared remarks, and slides. This also includes training material governing the use, sharing, or access to the "software" or any data related to or collected by the face recognition software/technology, including the legal standard that is required before using the technology.

3. PRIVACY ASSESSMENTS
A copy of any data retention guidelines or policies or other materials evaluating or guiding the security of the "software" or the privacy of the data involved.

4. USE POLICIES
Please provide a copy of any policy directives, guidance documents, memoranda, training materials, or similar records governing the use and function of this technology for any law enforcement purpose. Responsive materials include all those related to data retention, permissible and impermissible use, and security standards.

5. USE LOGS
All logs documenting the use of and searched conducted using the face recognition system for the period from August 1, 2025 through February 23, 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Madison Kitch

To Whom It May Concern:

The following request is about Orchid—sometimes called Orchid Health https://www.orchidhealth.com/, Orchid Biosciences, Inc https://www.orchidhealth.com/terms-of-service, or Orchid Bioscience, Inc https://opencorporates.com/companies/us_ca/4530928. it's a company focused on fertility -- specifically, the traits and health of embryos. Its CEO is Noor Siddiqui.

According to business filings, one of its registered addresses is 4022 STIRRUP CREEK DR, Suite 312, DURHAM 27703 NC United States, which appears to be a laboratory setting.

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records (dated from January 1, 2019 to the time this request is processed):

- All tips and 911 audios/transcripts made from the address 4022 STIRRUP CREEK DR, DURHAM 27703 NC, or mentioning said address. Please include any subsequent CAD reports, incident reports, and investigative documents produced in connection to these calls.
- All body camera footage produced in any police responses to the address 4022 STIRRUP CREEK DR, DURHAM 27703 NC.
- All tips or complaints related to Orchid/Orchid Health/Orchid Biosciences/Orchid Bioscience.
- All investigative documents produced in relation to any inquiry involving Orchid/Orchid Health/Orchid Biosciences/Orchid Bioscience.

I respectfully request that my request is expedited, given the newsworthiness and high-profile nature of surrogacy abuse in the US. This WSJ article highlights the issue: https://www.wsj.com/us-news/chinese-billionaires-surrogacy-pregnancy-7fdfc0c3. It mentions that Peter Thiel's family office "has backed a chain of IVF clinics across Southeast Asia and a recently opened branch in Los Angeles." This is referring to GenPrime, but Orchid also receives funding from Peter Thiel. I also respectfully request that documents are released on a rolling basis.

I am a news reporter for WIRED (wired.com), so the requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Therefore, I request that any fees are waived. But in the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by email attachment if available.

If you need to contact me, you can reach me at caroline_haskins@wired.com, or here. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,
Caroline Haskins

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In 2018 a series of bombings were perpetuated by Mark Anthony Conditt. The investigation involved the FBI, the Austin Police Department, and the Texas Department of Public Safety. Austin Police Department is referenced as the leading agency by the FBI in publicly released documents which state “with the Austin Police Department (APD) as the lead law enforcement agency o n the case, the FBI worked together with the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF), the U.S. Postal Service and the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) to support the operation from a joint command center at the Combined Transportation Emergency Communications Center (CTECC), located in Austin, Texas.” (https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20(Final)/view p47 ) Additionally, the Texas Department of Public Safety has referred me to your department after they have claimed not to be in possession of responsive documents. (https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/mark-anthony-conditt-and-austin-bombing-texas-department-of-public-safety-204697/) 

I am requesting the following items:

- Audio or direct transcription of the full 25 minute “confession” video recording by Mark Anthony Conditt recovered by law enforcement after his death. The existence of this tape is public knowledge and has been publicly documented by Governor Greg Abbot, former Austin Police chief Brian Manley, and internal FBI files  -

     https://web.archive.org/web/20180323120234/https://www.statesman.com/news/breaking-austin-bombing-suspect-dies-police-close-official-says/KZmUAGvKlNazDr31EzeUzI/

     https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2018/03/22/austin-bomber-challenged-young-man-or-terrorist/
     
     https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20(Final)/view (p40)

- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt.

I ask that the fee be waived as this information is within the public interest and many documents were widely commented on despite them not being available to the public (namely the 25 minute recording) release and access of this information is crucial to fair and accurate reporting on this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Nate Zaffiro

communications.subject: Public Information Act Request :: R179586-022226
communications.communication:
Dear Nate Zaffiro: 
 
Thank you for your interest in the public records of the Austin Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 2/22/2026 and given the reference number R179586-022226 for tracking purposes. 
Please be advised that the Austin Police Department’s Central Records Division receives over 2500 requests for information a month. These requests are processed in the order received, and we strive to respond to each request promptly, as required by law. 
Please know that, as a result of the large volume of requests received, the anticipated timeframe for responding to your request may take 10 business days if it is simply an offense report you're requesting.  If you are requesting body cam, dash cam, or other related records your request could take up to 30 days. 
You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records requested. PLEASE NOTE: The Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Austin Public Records Center. 
Austin Police Department

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Alexandra Johnson (Jerusalem 1500)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into former State Department diplomat Alexandra Uteev "Alix" Johnson (August 9, 1946 – October 12, 2002). Her death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find her obituary at the following link: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/128883126/alexandra_uteev-johnson

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. An IHSC Record reflecting the total number of Narcan administrations at ICE detention centers during Fiscal Year 2023 and 2024, including a breakdown of the location and date (by month/year) if each administration, if it exists. 
2. An IHSC Record reflecting the total number of positive drug tests for ICE detainees by facility during Fiscal Year 23 & 24. 

An adequate search responsive to this request will include, at minimum, a tasking of the ICE Health Services Corps.

I respectfully request expedited processing of this request pursuant to 6 C.F.R. 5.5(e) and attest and declare under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746 that the following statements in support of this request are true and accurate to the best of my knowledge and understanding. 

1. I am a member of the media engaged in information dissemination. See, e.g., https://theintercept.com/staff/r-andrew-free/; https://theappeal.org/authors/andrew-free/; https://atlpresscollective.com/author/free/; https://assets.aclu.org/live/uploads/2024/06/2024-07-01-ICE-Detainee-Deaths.pdf. 
2. As part of my reporting on immigration detention, I have received records from multiple jurisdictions across the country reflecting overdoses requiring emergency medical attention in ICE detention centers. These records indicate a prevalence of illegal drug use and unauthorized use of prescribed medications within facilities operated by certain ICE contractors that fit certain characteristics. 
3. ICE and at least one contractor are aware of, but have failed to disclose to the public, the death of at least one migrant in an ICE detention center related to fentanyl/synthetic fentanyl equivalent overdose. 
4. Public records I have obtained from ICE and local jurisdictions appear to reflect a rise in the number of overdoses inside certain ICE detention centers over the course of the past several months. 
5. As reflected by the widespread use of illegal drugs that preceded an immigrant's death from fentanyl-laced substances inside an ICE detention center, uncontrolled proliferation of illegal drugs within ICE detention poses deadly risks to the lives and safety of people inside. 
6. I will make these records available to the public and publish at least one edited piece about them.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents relating to usage of vehicle-based Automated License Plate Recognition (ALPR) technology in the City of San Francisco. This EXCLUDES all ALPR technology belonging to the San Francisco Police Department, and instead focuses on non-law enforcement ALPR systems and technology. For City of San Francisco / SFMTA ALPR technology, we are requesting:

1) NDAs, MSAs, SOWs, and contracts relating to the purchase and maintenance of ALPR systems owned/operated by the City of San Francisco / SFMTA
2) ALPR policies, procedures, guides, and training materials 
3)  The names of agencies and organizations with which the City of San Francisco / SFMTA shared ALPR data in 2025
4) All "records of access" required by Sec. 1798.90.52(a) for the periods June 1st, 2024 until the date of processing this request (An audit log of ALPR queries, time/date, identity/role, agency, and type of data accessed will suffice). If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. 
5) Any MOU or data sharing agreement which allows San Francisco City / SFMTA ALPR records to be shared or queried by non-administrative city staff (e.g. federal agencies, SFPD, etc.).
6) Any search warrants, subpoenas, or court orders you have received for ALPR data in 2025. 

Previously, I submitted a FOIA to BAAQMD based on finding ALPR cameras on a vehicle with their logo on it. They referred me to the City of San Francisco, stating that you own the vehicle. The City of San Francisco stated that SFMTA had responsive documents. For more context, you may read my exchange with them and view a photo of the vehicle in question here: https://www.muckrock.com/foi/california-52/baaqmd-alpr-review-189686/

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Concerning Flock Safety, these files;

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Typically the output for these audits is available in monthly batches.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Traci Phelps

communications.subject: Re: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
Hello,
This correspondence is in response to your Sunshine Law request, reference Sunshine #70 TRACI PHELPS.
After researching the request, the estimated amount of time required to fulfill this request is approximately 4 hours. Pursuant to Section 610.026, RSMo, the cost for staff time is $21.00 per hour, for a total estimated cost of $84.00.
Section 610.026, RSMo, authorizes public governmental bodies to charge for the actual cost of document research, duplication, and staff time required to fulfill a Sunshine Law request.
Please be advised that any records released will be reviewed and may require redaction of information exempt from disclosure under Missouri law, including but not limited to information related to active investigations, confidential records, or other sensitive information as permitted by law.
If you wish to proceed with this request, please remit payment in the amount of $84.00 to our office and reference Sunshine #70 TRACI PHELPS. Upon receipt of payment, processing of your request will begin.
If you choose not to proceed, no further action will be taken. Should you have any questions regarding this estimate, please feel free to contact our office.
Thank you,
